_README: WORKED EXAMPLE — copy this shape for your own cases. Seed a real one with `e2e.seed_calibration_case` (it pre-fills the judge's grades and leaves `human` blank), then fill `human`. This example shows the point of calibration: a deliberate human-vs-judge DISAGREEMENT on finding f2. The judge marked the Minnesota burial 'true', but the human reviewed the agent's tree and judged it only 'partial' (right place, wrong/garbled date) — that disagreement is exactly what calibration exists to surface. `final_tree`/`final_research` here are illustrative, not from a real run.
research_question: When and where did Kenneth Werner Quass die, and where was he buried?

findings.description: Kenneth Werner Quass died on 17 September 1982 in Grapevine, Tarrant County, Texas.
Kenneth Werner Quass was buried on 25 September 1982 in Madelia, Watonwan County, Minnesota.

proof_summaries.exhaustive_search_summary: Texas death index + Find a Grave consulted.
proof_summaries.narrative_markdown: Kenneth Werner Quass died 17 Sep 1982 at Grapevine, Texas (Texas Death Index) and was buried at Madelia, Minnesota (Find a Grave). Single index source for the burial.

notes: Disagreement on f2: agent recorded the right burial PLACE (Madelia, MN) but only a year-precision, less-specific date than the expected 25 Sep 1982. Judge called it a full match ('true'); human downgraded to 'partial'. This is a date-precision boundary case — exactly the kind of disagreement that should drive a judge_prompt tweak if it recurs.
